existing_features.title: Extend the Essay rich-text model to support heading levels (at minimum H1 and H2) so that an essay block can be rendered/stored distinctly as an H1 vs H2 heading.

Update the admin Essay editor toolbar to provide WYSIWYG controls for H1 and H2 headings in addition to the existing Bold/Italic controls, allowing the user to convert the current paragraph (or selected block) into an H1 or H2 heading and back to a paragraph.
Update RichText rendering to display heading blocks using the correct HTML heading tag and styling based on the stored heading level (H1 renders as <h1>, H2 renders as <h2>).
Update Markdown export to emit the correct number of leading '#' characters for headings based on heading level (H1 -> '#', H2 -> '##').

new_feature_requests.summary: Add basic WYSIWYG/rich-text editing for Essays (bold, italic, H1/H2, etc.)
Add a state migration for existing canister data if required by the heading-level schema change, ensuring upgrade does not trap and existing essays/pages remain accessible.

projectName: Add basic WYSIWYG heading controls (H1/H2) to Essay editor
